title: Graphorkis macrorhiza
taxonomy: Graphorkis macrorhiza is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This genus belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Gastrodieae. uses: Graphorkis macrorhiza is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. It is reported to have antidiabetic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential medicinal uses. It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
